name: Super Double-Cream Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 8.5 oz (227 g) chocolate wafer cookies, crushed
3 (8 oz or 225 g) packages cream cheese, softened
1 and 1/2 tablespoons bourbon or dark rum
recipeInstructions:
1. MAKE THE CRUST: In a food processor or blender, grind the cookies into moderately fine crumbs. Add sugar, cinnamon, and salt; process briefly to blend. Transfer to a bowl, pour in melted butter, and toss with a fork to moisten evenly. Press gently into the bottom and sides of a 9-inch springform pan.
2. PREPARE THE CHEESECAKE FILLING: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). In a food processor, combine cream cheese, sugar, salt, eggs; blend until smooth, scraping sides as needed. Add sour cream, lemon juice, bourbon, vanilla, and butter; blend. Pour into the cookie crumb shell and bake in the middle of the oven for 45 minutes.
3. Turn off the oven, prop door slightly open, and allow cake to rest in the oven for 1 hour. Cool on a rack and refrigerate at least 8 hours before serving.
Note: If the food processor container is not large enough, add only 1 cup of sour cream. Pour about half the mixture into a mixing bowl; add remaining sour cream to the processor and blend. Combine both batches in the mixing bowl and stir to blend.
